title: Postpartum Depression: Bipolar & the Truth About Motherhood with Eden Judd
summary: What happens when the “happiest time of your life” becomes the darkest? This week, Kate and Tansy sit down with former police officer and mum of one (and one on the way), Eden Judd, for a raw, unflinching conversation about postnatal depression, anxiety, bipolar II, and the brutal truths of early motherhood that no one talks about on Instagram. Eden shares how a traumatic birth, insomnia, and a crippling fear of asking for help led to a breakdown, and how she fought her way back through the doors of a Mother Baby Unit.  From the fear of having her child taken away to the deep stigma surrounding psychiatric medication during pregnancy, Eden opens up about what it really takes to survive and recover.Kate shares her own experience with perinatal PTSD, while Tansy dives into why judgment in motherhood is rampant—and why so many women feel they have to perform their way through pain. You'll learn: Why sleep is not a luxury in the postpartum period—it’s a survival strategy How the system is failing new mothers, and what real care should look like Why we need to stop judging mums who ask for help (and start showing up with lasagna) Whether you're a mum, a support person, or someone navigating mental health while raising a family, this conversation is a must-watch.  Brutal, tender, funny, and deeply necessary. Resources linked below for anyone who needs support.  COPE (Centre of Perinatal Excellence) – info & screening tools https://www.cope.org.au PANDA – helpline for perinatal anxiety & depression https://www.panda.org.au Gidget Foundation Australia – specialist care for new parents https://www.gidgetfoundation.org.au MumSpace (Parent‑Infant Research Institute) – free online mental health programs https://www.mumspace.com.au Peach Tree Perinatal Wellness – emotional support for new mums https://www.peachtree.org.au Lifeline – crisis support 24/7 https://www.lifeline.org.au #postnataldepression #bipolar2disorder #motherbabyunit #maternalmentalhealth #perinatalmentalhealth #ivfpregnancy #postpartumrecovery #bluntandbeautiful #kateforster #mentalhealthawareness #mumsupport #breakingthestigma

title: Understanding Mars in Astrology: Power, Drive & Desire Explained: The Astrology Fix
summary: Welcome back to The Astrology Fix, where we explore the celestial forces that shape our inner worlds. In this episode, Kate and Tansy dive deep into the planet Mars - the astrological firestarter that governs action, anger, desire, and drive. Mars gets a bad rap. It’s often blamed for impulsive decisions, aggression, or anger outbursts. But as Kate explains, Mars is not chaos. Mars is clarity. It's the part of you that says: "No more people-pleasing. This doesn't work for me anymore." Mars Sign: Tells you how you act. Your style of anger, desire, assertion. Mars House: Tells you where you need to act. The area of life that demands your fire. Some highlights: Mars in Leo: Needs pride and purpose. Overperforms when stressed. Doesn’t need permission to shine. Mars in Pisces: Intuitive and empathetic, but needs boundaries or risks burnout, dissociation, or addiction. Mars in the 6th House: Grows through rituals, routines, and structure. Without it? Perfectionism and burnout. Mars in the 11th House: Collective change lights your fire. Isolation dims it. They talk about Mars as a gut feeling, a tightening in your chest, the moment you say yes when you meant no. It’s your internal side coach, whispering: "You don't like that. Say no." title: Fashion, Neurodiversity & Finding Your Voice — with Melanie Dale (@letsgetlayered)
summary: In this episode, Kate and Tansy sit down with the unstoppable Melanie Dale, the creative powerhouse behind Let’s Get Layered, for one of the most honest, raw, and unexpectedly funny conversations about fashion, self-expression, and neurodiversity you’ll hear this year. Mel opens up about her late autism diagnosis, how fashion became both her armour and her voice, and what it’s really like navigating the online world as a woman who refuses to blend in. From op-shop treasures and Vivienne Westwood’s legacy to fast fashion, mental health, and the outrageous pricing at charity stores — nothing is off limits. They talk about the beauty and burnout of social media, the blurred lines between self-expression and self-preservation, and the freedom that comes with dressing for yourself — even if people think you look like Shrek in green (Mel’s words, not ours). And in true Blunt & The Beautiful style, they cover: Why fashion is inherently political What it’s really like living with autism and ADHD in a beige-wearing world The emotional toll of online hate and “outrage bots”How creativity becomes survival Why Kmart jeans might be the unsung hero of 2024 Plus, Mel gifts Kate a spider-themed handbag — and yes, it’s exactly as unhinged and perfect as it sounds. Key Takeaways? Fashion can be a form of therapy, rebellion, and communication. Neurodiversity and creativity often go hand in hand — but so does exhaustion. The fast-fashion cycle isn’t just bad for the planet, it’s bad for people. True self-expression means not needing validation — from strangers or comment sections. Sometimes the best looks are made from cat-piss vintage. title: Ghost Stories, Gut Feelings & the Spirit of Sisterhood
summary: Season three kicks off with a chill — and a little witchy wisdom. Kate and Tansy welcome bestselling author and journalist Karina Machado (Spirit Sisters, Where Spirits Dwell, Love Never Dies) for a conversation that walks the line between the seen and unseen. From a 1950s plumber who swore he saw Roman soldiers marching through a wall, to childhood seances, ancestral whispers, and intuitive downloads, the trio explore why some stories stay with us — and why women might just be more tuned‑in than we’ve ever admitted. Karina shares how her Uruguayan‑Spanish roots, a life‑changing walk along the Camino de Santiago, and decades of interviewing ordinary people with extraordinary experiences have deepened her belief that intuition, ancestry, and energy are all part of the same tapestry. Kate and Tansy add their own encounters (yes, there’s a Victorian ghost girl in a bath scene) and unpack why modern women are reclaiming their intuition — not as superstition, but as science, spirit, and self‑trust. It’s the perfect Halloween episode: spooky, soulful, and strangely comforting.
